summary: Iteration 25 — full E2E verification of new admin Card Operations (Team Boost seed + admin grant + open-all-pre-knockout) and the round-leaderboard backend powering the TopOfRound component. Backend: 11/11 pytest tests pass against the live preview backend (seed idempotency, list contains both boosts at correct prices/effect_types, grant happy-path + 404 for bad card / bad email, open-all response shape with locked_stages and no-leak into r32/r16/qf/sf/finals via before/after snapshot, round-leaderboard valid response with all required fields and rank ordering, invalid/empty round returns empty leaderboard with no 500). Frontend admin UI: all 8 required data-testids render under the dashboard tab; Seed button produces 'Team Boost cards seeded · 2 entries'; Grant form posts and shows 'Granted "Team Boost ×2" × 1 to 1 user(s)'. No JSX parse errors. Top-of-Round component code verified end-to-end against backend response shape, but a live in-page render could not be visually verified because the admin account does not own a fantasy main squad (MyTeamView returns 'Team not found' for non-owner squad IDs).

verified_endpoints.GET /api/admin/wc/cards/list: 200, contains card-team-boost-2x (10000 / team_boost_2x) and card-team-boost-3x (30000 / team_boost_3x)
verified_endpoints.POST /api/admin/wc/cards/grant: 200, granted=[{email,qty,user_id}], 404 on unknown card_id, 404 on unknown email
verified_endpoints.POST /api/admin/wc/games/open-all: 200, response keys = ok/flipped/skipped_started/open_before/open_after/locked_stages=['r32','r16','qf','sf','finals']; before/after snapshot confirms locked stages untouched
verified_endpoints.GET /api/fantasy/leaderboard/round?roun: 200, leaderboard rows include rank/user_id/squad_id/squad_name/display_name/country_code/round_points, sorted by round_points desc, matches_in_round=18 squads_scanned populated
200, leaderboard=[] (no crash, matches_in_round key omitted in empty path — see review comment)

action_items: (Low) When /api/fantasy/leaderboard/round returns empty leaderboard, the response omits matches_in_round and squads_scanned (the early-return branches only emit {round, leaderboard:[]}). The TopOfRound component already defaults these to 0 client-side, so it is non-blocking — but the original spec asked for 'matches_in_round=0' explicitly. Consider returning matches_in_round=0 and squads_scanned=0 in both empty branches for response-shape consistency.

(Low) Visual-editor instrumentation injects a <span x-excluded style='display:contents'> inside <option> elements of the grant-card-select, which triggers a React hydration warning 'In HTML, <span> cannot be a child of <option>'. This is from the build-time instrumentation, not application code, so it should not affect production builds. If it persists in prod, the fix is to render plain text in <option> children instead of conditional JSX expressions.

(Optional) Visually confirm TopOfRound by signing in as a regular user that owns a main squad (admin@cloudypitch.com doesn't own one — MyTeamView returns 'Team not found' for non-owner squad IDs). Backend payload + component code already verified.
critical_code_review_comments: wc_games.py open-all: cleanly filters with $nin LOCKED_STAGES, then per-game kickoff/closes_at check is sound. Audit-log entry written with full metadata (flipped, skipped_started, scanned, open_before, open_after, locked_stages). Good.
wc_games.py admin_seed_team_boost_cards: uses upsert with $setOnInsert created_at and $set updated_at — properly idempotent.

wc_games.py admin_grant_cards: validates card existence (404), validates at least 1 matching user (404), inserts uses_remaining=card.starter_uses with obtained_via='admin_grant', writes audit_log with recipients list. Solid.

fantasy.py fantasy_round_leaderboard: 161-line single function but logically chunked (resolve matches → bulk events/lineups → bulk players → per-squad loop → hydrate users → return). Captain ×2 with vice fallback is correct. Could memoize per-(match,player) point calc if perf becomes an issue with many squads, but n≤5000 squads × n_matches is fine for now.
TopOfRound.jsx is concise (96 lines), uses ISO-2 flag codepoint trick (no image bundle), has alive-flag cleanup for stale fetches. Good.
Admin.jsx GrantCardForm: ResponsesetCardId based on the first card-team-boost-2x match on initial load — convenient default. Validates non-empty card + emails before submit. Clean.

Note: returned data-testid pattern for individual top-of-round rows isn't present (only the wrapper 'top-of-round-leaderboard' has a data-testid). Adding per-row data-testid='top-of-round-row-{rank}' would help future automated tests verify row count + ordering.

success_rate.frontend: 100% on admin UI flows; TopOfRound visual mount not validated due to admin not owning a squad (backend payload + component code reviewed and correct)

seed_data_creation: Team Boost ×2 (card-team-boost-2x) and Team Boost ×3 (card-team-boost-3x) are now seeded in legend_cards via /api/admin/wc/cards/seed-team-boosts; one card-team-boost-2x was granted to admin@cloudypitch.com (user_cards row inserted, audit log written) — both are intentional test fixtures, safe to leave.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All iteration-25 endpoints validated. Backend pytest is /app/backend/tests/test_iteration25.py — reusable as regression. To visually validate TopOfRound, sign in as a user that owns a main WC2026 squad (admin doesn't), navigate to /my-teams/main/<their_squad_id>, scroll below pitch — component is conditionally rendered only when isMain && rounds.length > 0. The fantasy round-leaderboard backend already returns populated data (5 rows for 'Matchday 1' with matches_in_round=18 in current DB).
rca of the issue: n/a — no blocking bugs found. Two low-priority polish items captured in action_items.
